metadata.targetApp: Autonomous algorithmic trading operator console for Indian equity markets

primaryAudience.audience: Developer reviewing autonomous decisions and governance evidence
Risk reviewer reconstructing what happened after a session

metadata.mission: Redesign the existing server-rendered operator console into a professional, explainable trading control room. The UI must answer three questions immediately: what is happening, why did the system do it, and what evidence supports it.

codexDirective.instruction: Use this XML as the product and UX specification for implementing the new UI.
Preserve the current architecture: Node.js native http servers, TypeScript SSR HTML strings, no SPA, no client-side router, no React/Vue/Svelte, and no frontend bundler.
Work inside the existing src/operator-ui area unless a small read-model addition is required.
Use existing read models and database evidence wherever possible. Do not invent trade reasons, promotion reasons, PnL, confidence, or strategy outcomes.
When evidence is unavailable, render an explicit missing-evidence state with the exact field or table needed.
All dynamic HTML must still be server-rendered and delivered through existing routes or /api/refresh fragments.
Escape all user, model, broker, and database-provided text before rendering.

currentState.item: The operator console exists and has dashboard, positions, strategies, decisions, governance, health, and detail pages.
The UI is functional but not sufficiently explanatory for a high-stakes autonomous trading system.
Operators cannot quickly understand what trades were made, why they were made, why a strategy was promoted, why a strategy won, or why another strategy lost.

Evidence exists across strategy_decisions, execution_attempts, paper_orders, paper_fills, paper_positions, risk_events, strategy_lifecycle_state, governance_decisions, strategy_runs, walk_forward_runs, walk_forward_windows, walk_forward_trials, research_artifacts, and related tables.

desiredState.item: Every important trading and governance action has a plain-English narrative, a timeline, and supporting evidence.
The dashboard shows market mode, execution mode, broker status, risk posture, active positions, recent trades, promoted strategies, blocked actions, and open operator concerns.
Decision detail pages reconstruct the full chain from proposal to validation to risk gate to execution to fill to outcome.
Strategy pages explain the strategy thesis, current lifecycle state, performance evidence, promotion or demotion criteria, and competitor comparison.
Governance pages show why strategies moved between backtest, paper, and live phases.
The design looks professional, dense enough for operators, but not cryptic.

nonGoals.nonGoal: Do not redesign the trading algorithms or broker integration.
Do not add live execution actions beyond existing safe actions.
Do not convert the app into an SPA.
Do not add a heavy CSS framework unless the repository already uses one.
Do not create promotional trading advice language. Use operational and evidentiary language.

architectureConstraints.constraint: Node.js 22 or newer, TypeScript strict, ES2022, NodeNext.
Native http.createServer only. No Express/Fastify unless already present and approved elsewhere.
Server-rendered HTML string templates in src/operator-ui.
Use shared CSS in render-utils.ts or a small operator-ui style helper. Keep CSS variables and responsive breakpoints.
Use progressive enhancement only: server-side polling through /api/refresh and fragment replacement. No mandatory JS framework.
Respect existing HTTP Basic Auth, rate limiting, and lockout behavior.
Prefer existing DashboardReadModel, OperatorReadModel, and detail read models. Add read-model fields only when required for explainability.
Show execution mode and risk guard state on every page.

userExperiencePrinciples.principle: Every trade, block, promotion, demotion, and winner selection must have a visible reason and evidence trail.
The top of each page must communicate current risk, market status, and whether the operator needs to act.
Visual polish must never hide IDs, timestamps, rule names, or source evidence.
Use words like evidence, score, passed, failed, and threshold. Avoid claiming certainty.
Show a concise summary first, then expandable evidence details, raw IDs, and source records.
Use calm, concise, institutional language. Avoid hype, emojis, and informal phrasing.
A blocked trade must look different from a paper trade and a live trade. A promoted strategy must look different from a candidate.

navItem.label: Overview
navItem.purpose: Command-center snapshot of current trading, risk, performance, research, and open issues.

navItem.label: Trades & Decisions
navItem.purpose: Decision inbox with explanation-first summaries and filters.

navItem.label: Positions
navItem.purpose: Open exposure, PnL, risk concentration, and why each position exists.

navItem.label: Strategies
navItem.purpose: Strategy leaderboard, lifecycle state, winner/loser reasoning, and performance evidence.

navItem.label: Governance
navItem.purpose: Promotion, demotion, threshold checks, and approval history.

navItem.label: Research
navItem.purpose: Overnight hypotheses, artifacts, lineage, and candidate funnel. If no route exists, expose as a dashboard section first.

navItem.label: System Health
navItem.purpose: Broker, DB, auth, scheduler, token, and subsystem diagnostics.

globalHeader.element: Environment badge: blocked, paper, or live
Market phase badge: PreMarket, Regular, PostMarket, Closed
Risk guard status and kill-switch state

pageHierarchy.level: What is the system doing now?
What evidence supports or contradicts it?
What changed since the previous decision or strategy run?

visualDirection.density: Medium-high information density with strong section hierarchy
visualDirection.avoid: Neon gamer styling, oversized empty cards, hidden raw evidence, ambiguous colors

color.name: danger
color.usage: Failed, blocked, risk breach, live danger

font.name: ui
font.value: Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, Segoe UI, sans-serif

font.name: mono
font.value: JetBrains Mono, SFMono-Regular, Consolas, Liberation Mono, monospace

field.name: openPositions
field.source: paper_positions or live position read model

component.emptyState: Show Unknown and a missing-evidence note. Do not hide the strip.

component.purpose: Plain-English explanation for a trade, block, promotion, demotion, or winner selection.

element.name: title
element.example: Why this BUY order was placed

element.name: summarySentence
element.example: The system placed a paper BUY for RELIANCE because Strategy v3 generated a validated long proposal, exposure stayed below the configured cap, and no duplicate order was detected.

element.name: evidencePills
element.examples: Validator passed, Risk allowed, Paper fill recorded, Stop-loss attached

element.name: sourceLinks
element.examples: Decision ID, execution attempt ID, paper order ID, fill ID, strategy run ID

rules.rule: Use because language only when source evidence exists.
If source evidence is missing, say Evidence not captured yet rather than guessing.
For failed or blocked decisions, title must start with Why this was blocked or Why this was not executed.

component.purpose: Show pass/fail/warn status for validation, risk, governance, and promotion criteria.

rendering.item: Pass rows use green badge and should not dominate the page.
Fail rows use red badge and appear first in failure contexts.
Missing rows use muted warning styling with implementation hint.

component.purpose: Reconstruct proposal to outcome in chronological order.
component.emptyState: Render missing steps as Not reached or Evidence not captured.

component.purpose: Explain why a strategy is winning, losing, promoted, demoted, or held.

field.name: performance
field.examples: Sharpe, drawdown, win rate, total return, window count, replay fidelity

field.name: risk
field.examples: max drawdown, exposure, concentration, blocked orders, loss-limit proximity

component.purpose: Show why a strategy won versus other candidates and why losing strategies lost.

rules.rule: Sort winners first, then active candidates, then rejected candidates.
For losers, show the first decisive failing criterion and at least one supporting metric.
Do not label a strategy loser only because it ranked lower; explain the exact metric or gate that caused rejection.

component.purpose: Make safety state visible before performance.
Prevent false confidence when a reason cannot be reconstructed.
component.copy: Evidence not captured for this step. Add this field to the read model or link the relevant source record before rendering a narrative.

page.title: Overview
page.goal: Give the operator a complete answer to what is happening now and what needs attention.

section.title: Trading Control Room
section.content: Show current environment, market phase, execution mode, risk posture, broker status, and last tick.

section.title: Recent trade decisions
section.description: Show the last 5 to 10 decisions as explanation cards, not just rows.
section.cardFields: symbol, side, quantity, mode, status, primary reason, strategy, risk result, execution result, time, detail link

section.title: Strategy movement
section.description: Show strategies promoted, demoted, held, or rejected in the latest governance cycle.

section.title: Blocked or degraded actions
section.description: Surface blocked orders, failed token refresh, risk breaches, stale data, or missing evidence.
section.priority: High visibility when non-empty. Collapse when empty.

section.title: Overnight research funnel
section.description: Hypotheses generated, deduplicated, rejected, evaluated, selected, and published.
section.fields: hypothesesGenerated, duplicatesRejected, validationFailures, walkForwardRuns, selectedWinner, publicationStatus

acceptance.criterion: Operator can identify execution mode and risk state without scrolling.
At least the latest 5 decisions include plain-English why summaries or missing-evidence callouts.
Promotions and demotions include threshold evidence.

page.title: Trades & Decisions
page.goal: Turn the existing recent decisions table into a decision inbox with filters and explanations.

listItem.links: decision detail, strategy detail, backtest run

page.emptyState: No decisions match the current filters.

acceptance.criterion: Blocked decisions are visually distinct from executed decisions.
Rows include reasons without opening the detail page.

page.title: Decision Detail
page.goal: Provide forensic reconstruction of one autonomous trade decision.

section.title: Why this decision happened
section.requiredData: decision id, symbol, side, strategy id, strategy version, validator result, risk result, execution result, order or blocked reason

section.title: Decision timeline
section.requiredData: timestamps and source record ids for proposal, validation, risk, execution, fill, position, outcome

section.title: Strategy context
section.fields: strategy thesis, lifecycle state, current rank, latest walk-forward evidence, governance state, link to strategy page

section.title: Market context at decision time
section.fields: quote, spread if available, price movement, market phase, instrument, liquidity proxy, data freshness
section.fallback: If not available, show missing evidence and suggested source zerodha_latest_quotes or broker quote snapshot.

section.title: Validation and risk checks
section.requiredChecks: IndiaProposalValidator result, duplicate order guard, exposure cap, max open positions, daily loss limit, kill switch, mode gate

section.title: Execution evidence
section.fields: mode, order id, broker order id if live, paper order id, fill id, fill price, slippage estimate, stop-loss, trailing-stop, error message

section.title: Outcome so far
section.fields: position status, realized PnL, unrealized PnL, latest quote, stop-loss state, position events

section.title: Raw evidence
section.fields: source table names, record IDs, JSON payload excerpts with safe escaping, created_at timestamps
section.rendering: Use collapsed details blocks by default.

copyTemplates.template: Paper BUY executed because {strategy} generated a validated long proposal for {symbol}, risk checks passed, and the paper execution gate accepted the order.
Order was blocked because {riskRule} failed: observed {observedValue}, allowed {threshold}.
Order was not sent to market because execution mode is blocked. The decision was recorded for audit only.
Proposal failed validation because {validatorReason}. No execution attempt should be made.
The system recorded the decision, but the reason cannot be reconstructed from the current read model.

page.title: Positions
page.goal: Explain current exposure and why each position exists.

section.title: Open positions
section.columns: symbol, quantity, avg cost, latest price, unrealized PnL, exposure, stop-loss, trailing-stop, age, strategy, why open, risk state, detail links
section.rowNarrative: {symbol} remains open because {strategy} opened it on {date}; current PnL is {pnl}; stop-loss is {stop}; latest risk event is {riskEvent}.

section.title: Position event timeline
section.description: For selected or recent positions, show fills, stop updates, partial exits, and risk events.

section.title: Concentration and limits
section.fields: top symbol exposure, sector if available, strategy exposure, max exposure, daily PnL limit utilization

acceptance.criterion: Every open position links back to the decision that created or modified it.
Positions with missing origin decision show a MissingEvidenceCallout.

page.title: Strategies
page.goal: Make strategy quality and lifecycle state understandable without reading raw metrics.

section.title: Strategy leaderboard
section.description: Rank active and candidate strategies by latest governance score or walk-forward result.

section.title: Active strategies
section.cardFields: strategy id, version, lifecycle state, thesis, promoted because, last decision, paper/live PnL, risk notes, detail link

section.title: Candidate strategies
section.cardFields: rank, candidate thesis, backtest score, walk-forward status, failed criteria, next required evidence

section.title: Why strategies lost
section.description: List rejected/demoted strategies with decisive failure criteria and comparison to the winner.
section.fields: strategy, failed threshold, observed value, winner value, governance reason, run id

acceptance.criterion: The winning strategy has an explicit primary reason and threshold evidence.
Each losing strategy has a specific failure reason.

page.title: Strategy Detail
page.goal: Explain one strategy like an investment committee memo, backed by audit evidence.

section.title: {strategyId} v{strategyVersion}
section.fields: lifecycle state, thesis, current rank, latest governance action, promoted/demoted/held reason

section.title: Promotion criteria
section.checks: Sharpe threshold, drawdown threshold, window count threshold, replay fidelity threshold, validation status, paper performance if applicable, risk event count
section.copy: Promoted because all required criteria passed. Held because at least one criterion is missing or below threshold. Demoted because a hard safety or performance criterion failed.

section.title: Performance over time
section.description: Use compact server-rendered tables or SVG sparklines if existing data supports it.
section.fields: returns by window, drawdown by window, rank by run, PnL by period

section.title: Trades made by this strategy
section.fields: decision, symbol, side, reason, execution status, PnL, link

section.title: Research lineage
section.fields: hypothesis id, generation attempt, artifact links, publication status, dedupe status, failure memory if rejected

section.title: Backtests and walk-forward runs
section.fields: run id, trial id, window count, metrics, selected winner flag, link to /backtest

page.title: Governance
page.goal: Show why strategies moved through backtest, paper, and live lifecycle states.

section.title: Latest governance cycle
section.fields: cycle time, selected winner, promoted strategies, demoted strategies, held strategies, rejected strategies, operator-impact summary

section.title: Promotion board
section.description: Compare candidates side by side with pass/fail evidence.

section.title: Lifecycle timeline
section.fields: strategy, previous state, new state, reason, triggering run, metrics, timestamp

section.title: Governance thresholds
section.description: Show required thresholds and observed values for selected cycle.

copyTemplates.template: Promoted from {fromState} to {toState} because {passedCount} of {totalCount} required criteria passed, including Sharpe {sharpe} versus threshold {sharpeThreshold} and max drawdown {drawdown} versus limit {drawdownLimit}.
Held in {state} because {failedCriterion} did not meet the threshold: observed {observedValue}, required {threshold}.
Demoted from {fromState} to {toState} because {reason}. This should reduce execution exposure until new evidence is available.

page.title: Research
page.goal: Make overnight LLM research auditable and understandable.
page.implementationNote: If adding a new route is too large, implement these sections on the dashboard first and create the route later.

section.title: Hypothesis funnel
section.steps: generated, validated, duplicate rejected, backtested, walk-forward evaluated, selected, published, promoted

section.title: Hypotheses
section.fields: hypothesis id, thesis, source prompt/run, validation status, duplicate status, exact failure memory match, evaluation status, strategy created

section.title: Research artifacts
section.fields: artifact id, title, strategy link, publication time, evaluation summary, failure notes

page.title: Backtest Detail
page.goal: Explain why a backtest or walk-forward run did or did not support promotion.

section.title: Run summary
section.fields: run id, strategy, date range, windows, trials, selected winner, score, errors

section.title: Walk-forward windows
section.fields: window, train period, test period, return, Sharpe, drawdown, trade count, pass/fail

section.title: Governance impact
section.description: Show whether this run caused a promotion, demotion, hold, or no lifecycle change.

page.title: System Health
page.goal: Make runtime health operationally useful, not just technical.

section.title: Health summary
section.fields: overall readiness, liveness, scheduler, broker, database, UI auth, Upstox token, MCP, last errors

section.title: Broker token and market data
section.fields: token status, expiry if available, last refresh, manual refresh action, quote staleness, instrument sync age

section.title: Subsystem evidence
section.fields: broker supervisor, proposal supervisor, execution supervisor, strategy risk supervisor, universe supervisor, overnight supervisor

section.title: Operator auth
section.fields: rate limiting status, lockout threshold, recent auth failures without exposing secrets

safety.rule: Never render tokens, secrets, TOTP seeds, API keys, passwords, or authorization headers.

contract.description: Canonical shape for rendering trade decision explanation cards and detail pages.

source.purpose: proposal payload and LLM validation lineage

contract.description: Canonical shape for explaining strategy promotion, demotion, winning, and losing.

field.name: primaryReason
field.rule: Must come from governance_decisions or derived threshold checklist.

pattern.description: Continue using /api/refresh to return JSON plus HTML fragments.

requirements.requirement: Each refreshable section must have a stable data-fragment-id.
When a fragment update fails, show stale data with a stale badge rather than blanking the section.
Show last refreshed time per page and per critical fragment when available.

pattern.description: Use native details/summary blocks for raw evidence and long JSON payloads.

requirements.requirement: Default expanded for failed checks and blocked reasons.

pattern.description: Use server-rendered query-parameter filters on decisions, strategies, and governance pages.

requirements.requirement: Filters must work without client-side JS.
Preserve selected filters in query params.

rules.rule: Prefer short explanatory sentences over raw labels.
Use exact timestamps with timezone where possible.
Use IDs in muted monospace near human labels.
Use INR formatting consistently for notional, PnL, and exposure.
Negative PnL must be visually distinguishable and include sign.
Never say guaranteed, safe, certain, or best unless it is part of a stored source quote and clearly labeled.
For live mode, show a persistent red live badge and risk warning.

accessibility.requirement: All status badges must include text, not color only.
Use semantic headings in order: h1, h2, h3.
Tables must include th elements and meaningful captions or section titles.
Keyboard focus must be visible for links, buttons, and details summaries.
Use aria-live="polite" only for refresh status text, not entire tables.
Respect reduced motion; avoid animated flashing in risk states.

securityAndSafety.requirement: Escape all dynamic text with existing escapeHtml utility.
Do not render broker tokens, API secrets, passwords, TOTP codes, bearer tokens, cookies, or auth headers.
Manual token refresh buttons must preserve existing POST routes and authentication behavior.
For any future destructive or live-trading control, require explicit confirmation and server-side authorization. Do not add such controls in this redesign unless already implemented.
When executionMode is live, the UI must show a persistent live trading indicator above page content.

phase.name: Design foundation
phase.task: Refactor or extend render-utils.ts with the new layout shell, status strip, cards, badges, evidence checklist, timeline, and missing evidence components.
Keep existing formatter utilities for INR, numbers, dates, and escaping. Add small helpers only where needed.
Add CSS variables and responsive grid classes from this spec.

phase.name: Explanation data mapping
phase.task: Create adapter functions that transform existing dashboard/detail read-model payloads into TradeDecisionExplanation and StrategyGovernanceExplanation shapes.
Do not change database schema in the UI pass unless necessary. Prefer read-model joins and graceful missing evidence callouts.
Document every missing source field in code comments near the adapter.

phase.name: Page upgrades
phase.task: Upgrade / with status strip, risk posture, recent trade narratives, strategy movement, blocked actions, and research funnel.
Upgrade /decisions with filters, explanation rows, badges, and links.
Upgrade /decision detail with WhyNarrativeCard, DecisionTimeline, validation/risk checklist, market context, execution evidence, outcome, and raw evidence.
Upgrade /strategies and /strategy detail with StrategyScorecard, WinnerLoserComparison, and promotion criteria.
Upgrade /governance with promotion board, lifecycle timeline, and threshold matrix.
Upgrade /positions with origin decision links and why-open narratives.
Upgrade /system-health with grouped health and safe token status display.

phase.name: Refresh fragments
phase.task: Update /api/refresh payloads to return fragments for new dashboard sections where the old dashboard had dynamic updates.
Keep old fragment IDs compatible where tests or clients depend on them. Add aliases rather than breaking existing IDs.

phase.name: Tests and proof
phase.task: Add or update Vitest tests for HTML rendering of explanation cards, missing evidence states, live-mode badge, and escaping.
Add tests for blocked decision copy, promoted strategy copy, loser failure reason copy, and no-secret rendering.
Run the existing operator UI proof/witness tests if present.

likelyFiles.file: src/operator-ui/pages/dashboard.ts or equivalent dashboard page file
src/operator-ui/pages/decisions.ts or equivalent decisions page file
src/operator-ui/pages/decision-detail.ts or equivalent decision detail page file
src/operator-ui/pages/positions.ts or equivalent positions page file
src/operator-ui/pages/strategies.ts or equivalent strategies page file
src/operator-ui/pages/strategy-detail.ts or equivalent strategy detail page file
src/operator-ui/pages/governance.ts or equivalent governance page file
src/operator-ui/pages/backtest-detail.ts or equivalent backtest detail page file
src/operator-ui/pages/system-health.ts or equivalent system health page file
src/operator-ui/read-models or equivalent detail read models

fileGuidance.rule: If current filenames differ, locate equivalent files by route and exported render function.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: The UI stays server-rendered and runs without a frontend build step.
The dashboard immediately shows execution mode, market phase, broker state, risk guard state, last tick, and last refresh.
The latest trade decisions show what trade was made or blocked and why.
The decision detail page reconstructs proposal, validation, risk, execution, fill, position, and outcome with timestamps or missing-evidence callouts.
The strategy pages explain why a strategy is active, promoted, held, rejected, demoted, winning, or losing.
The governance page shows threshold pass/fail evidence for lifecycle changes.
No secrets are rendered anywhere in the UI.
The UI is responsive at desktop, tablet, and mobile widths.
Tests cover rendering of key explanation states and security-sensitive output.

sampleRenderedCopy.copy: Paper BUY for {symbol} was recorded because {strategyId} v{strategyVersion} generated a validated proposal, the risk guard allowed the notional exposure, and the paper ledger recorded a fill at {fillPrice}.
This order was blocked because the duplicate-order guard found an existing open order for {symbol}. No broker order was sent.
{strategyId} v{strategyVersion} was promoted to {newState} because it passed {passedCount}/{totalCount} governance checks, including Sharpe {sharpe} and max drawdown {drawdown}.
{strategyId} v{strategyVersion} was not promoted because {failedCriterion} failed. Observed {observedValue}; required {threshold}.
Evidence is not available for {field}. Add {suggestedSource} to the read model before showing this explanation.

uiSpec.codexPrompt:
You are updating the Trader operator console UI/UX. Follow the XML spec exactly.
Preserve the existing Node.js native http, TypeScript, server-rendered HTML architecture. Do not introduce React, Vue, Angular, Svelte, a client-side router, or a frontend bundler.
First inspect src/operator-ui and identify the current route renderers and shared render utilities. Implement reusable SSR components for: - OperatorStatusStrip - WhyNarrativeCard - EvidenceChecklist - DecisionTimeline - StrategyScorecard - WinnerLoserComparison - RiskPosturePanel - MissingEvidenceCallout
Upgrade the existing routes: - / overview dashboard - /decisions decision inbox - /decision?id=... decision detail - /positions positions with why-open narratives - /strategies strategy leaderboard with winner/loser reasoning - /strategy?strategyId=...&strategyVersion=... strategy memo - /governance promotion/demotion board - /backtest?runId=... backtest explanation - /system-health grouped operational health
Use existing read models first. Build adapter functions that map available data into TradeDecisionExplanation and StrategyGovernanceExplanation shapes. If a reason cannot be reconstructed from existing data, render a MissingEvidenceCallout. Never invent reasons.
Keep /api/refresh working. Add stable fragment IDs for new dashboard sections. Preserve compatibility for existing fragments where possible.
Add tests for: - HTML escaping in all explanation components - live mode badge visibility - blocked decision explanation - paper fill explanation - promoted strategy threshold checklist - rejected strategy failure reason - missing evidence state - no tokens/secrets rendered in system health
Run the existing test suite relevant to operator UI and fix failures.
